brand.name: Con la tuya, contribuyente
brand.tagline: Every peso the Uruguayan state spends, traceable.

money.scaleHelp: The gold bar under each amount is logarithmic and uses the same scale across the site: you can compare at a glance without reading the digits.
money.primaryNote: Amounts are converted to Uruguayan pesos when the source reports a foreign currency. The original currency is shown.
money.mixedCurrency: This contract has amounts in more than one currency.
money.convertedFrom: Amount converted to pesos from {currency}, at the exchange rate on the award date.
money.todayHelp: The amount brought to today's pesos: foreign currency is converted at the BCU rate of the contract's month, then adjusted for inflation via the Unidad Indexada. Lets you compare prices from different years.

preview.convertAria: Show the price in pesos and in today's pesos

common.searchPlaceholder: Search by subject, supplier, or agency
common.name: Name

home.title: Follow the money.
home.lead: Every tender, award, and supplier of the Uruguayan state since {yearFrom}. Official open data, searchable.
home.ledgerHelp: What the state awarded most recently, according to the published data.
home.trendsHelp: Total awarded per year, in Uruguayan pesos.
home.anomaliesHelp: Contracts that depart from the usual pattern. A flag is not an accusation: it's a starting point for review.
home.sourceNote: Source: Compras Estatales open data (OCDS), catalogodatos.gub.uy.

gastos.title: Where your money goes.
gastos.lead: {count} public purchases by the Uruguayan state since {yearFrom}, grouped for what they are: who gets paid, who spends, what is bought, and what doesn't add up. All from the data the state itself publishes.

noTotal.title: The state can't give you a reliable total
noTotal.body: A handful of official records carry corrupt quantities. A single award declares 1,200,007 generation units at USD 519,788 each: USD 623 billion —about eight times the country's GDP— in one contract.
noTotal.body2: Three records like that hold 86% of all reported spending. A «total spent» headline would, in effect, be the sum of those errors. That's why the figure above is the median: half of the state's purchases are worth less than that, and it doesn't break on one bad number.

volume.title: How many purchases the state makes, by year
volume.help: Number of purchases recorded each year. The volume keeps growing; tap a bar to open that year in the explorer.

suppliers.title: Who gets the money
suppliers.help: The suppliers that were paid the most by the state. Fuel importers and printers lead the list.

buyers.title: Who spends it
buyers.help: The bodies that spend the most. Fuel, roads and energy account for most of it.
buyers.byType: See spending grouped by type of body

categories.title: What the state buys
categories.help: The categories with the most reported spending. Roads and fuel dominate the tab.

flags.title: What doesn't add up
flags.help: Purchases whose unit price spikes against what's usually paid for the same item. A flag is not an accusation: it's where to start looking.
flags.seeUnexplained: See the ones AI couldn't explain

opacity.title: The opacity, in two numbers
opacity.body: The state does publish its purchases, but incompletely. Auditing or filtering on this data leaves most records out.
opacity.noStatus: of tenders don't report how they ended
opacity.noMethod: don't report which procedure was used

explore.title: Now look for yourself
explore.body: This all comes from open data. You can drill down to each contract and its official record.

gastos.sourceNote: Source: Compras Estatales open data (OCDS), catalogodatos.gub.uy. Amounts normalized to pesos; a few records with impossible quantities are excluded from averages.

recop.indexLead: Public spending grouped by event: a festival, a public works push, a story. Each compilation gathers the contracts of a single event and adds up what it cost.
recop.teaserBody: Spending grouped by event — a festival, a public works push, a story — with its total cost and the contract-by-contract detail.
recop.suppliersHelp: Suppliers ranked by amount awarded in this event. A contract with several suppliers counts in full for each: a ceiling, not a split.
recop.categoriesHelp: Spending by category, from each awarded item's price times quantity, in the contract currency.
recop.ledgerHelp: Every contract in the event, largest first. Tap one for its record and the link to the official purchase.
recop.ledgerNote: Amounts in pesos, normalized. Records with impossible quantities are excluded from the total.
recop.methodBody: Each compilation is a saved query over the open data: a body, a text and, sometimes, a year range. The site resolves it live, so totals and detail are always current. The set of contracts is defined by editorial judgment, the way a journalist would.

notFound.title: We couldn't find that compilation
notFound.body: It may have been renamed or doesn't exist yet.

pauta.title: The state's advertising, and who collects it
pauta.lead: How much the Uruguayan state spends on advertising and propaganda, which bodies pay for it, and which media and agencies receive it. Amounts are apportioned per recipient, not the full contract.
pauta.outletsHelp: The biggest earners of state advertising: media, agencies and —sometimes— other public bodies. Amount apportioned per recipient within each ad type.
pauta.outletsNote: The list includes advertising agencies and the odd public body, not only media outlets. A recipient with a public-body name (e.g. a Secretaría) topping the ranking is a quirk of the official data worth a closer look: it may be an internal transfer or a misattributed record.
pauta.buyersHelp: The bodies that spend the most on advertising. Tourism, the state banks and gambling (lotteries and casinos) lead the list.
pauta.formatsHelp: The advertising-and-propaganda catalogue categories, by spend. «Advertising and broadcast service» accounts for almost all of it. Tap a category to see those contracts.
pauta.byYearHelp: Advertising-and-propaganda spend by year, in pesos.
pauta.sourceNote: Source: Compras Estatales open data (OCDS), «Advertising and Propaganda» catalogue class. Totals are exact; the rankings sum each category's main recipients and payers, not the long tail.
pauta.teaserBody: How much the state spends on advertising and who collects it: media, agencies and the bodies that advertise most.

sup.aiNote: Each supplier's category is AI-generated (Gemini) from the name: it's indicative, can be wrong, verify against the source.

dei.badgeTitle: Industrial company listed in the Directory of Industrial Companies (MIEM)
dei.title: Industrial registry (DEI)
dei.source: Source: Directory of Industrial Companies (DEI), MIEM — open data. The match to the supplier is by tax ID (RUT).

insights.title: Industrial companies that sell to the State
insights.summary: {matched} of the {total} registered industrial companies (DEI) are state suppliers.
insights.smallHelp: Companies registered as micro or small that rank among the largest state suppliers. Not an irregularity in itself, but the gap between declared size and awarded amount is worth a look.
insights.mapHelp: Each point is a registered industrial company that sells to the State, placed at its primary establishment.

rupe.title: State Suppliers Registry (RUPE)
rupe.source: Source: ARCE — Registro Único de Proveedores del Estado (RUPE), open data. Cross-referenced to the supplier by RUT.

estad.title: Public spending by the numbers
estad.lead: Uruguay's state spending in figures: how much, on what, to whom and —what no explorer gives you— by type of supplier.
estad.typesHelp: What kind of supplier the money goes to: companies, public bodies, media, agencies, co-ops or individuals. The category is inferred by AI from the name.
estad.typesNote: Across the {n} biggest suppliers by spend, already categorized. It does not cover the tail of thousands of small suppliers.
estad.volumeHelp: Number of purchases recorded each year.
estad.sourceNote: Source: Compras Estatales open data (OCDS). The median replaces the total (a few records with impossible quantities break any sum). Spending by type covers the AI-categorized suppliers.

mapa.title: The public spending map
mapa.lead: Everything the state spends, dimensioned: each body is a rectangle, its size is what it spent. Grouped by type of body.
mapa.hint: Each rectangle's area is proportional to spend. Tap a body for its profile; pick a group to zoom in.
mapa.note: Area = published procurement spend, normalized to pesos; records with impossible quantities are excluded. Precomputed monthly.
mapa.groupAria: Pick a group of public bodies

contracts.title: Contract explorer
contracts.lead: Filter {total} contracts by agency, supplier, amount, year, and procedure.
contracts.resultsHelp: Totals computed over the applied filters.
contracts.histogramHelp: Number of contracts per year within the applied filters.

empty.title: No contracts match these filters
empty.body: Remove a filter or widen the year range.

emptyInitial.title: Search or filter to start
emptyInitial.body: Type what the state bought, or pick an agency in the filter panel.

contracts.tooMany: Showing the first {shown}. Narrow the filters to see fewer results.
contracts.needFilter: Add a filter (year, agency, supplier or amount) to see totals.

filters.title: Filters
filters.searchHelp: Searches the tender subject, the items, the agency, and the supplier.
filters.statusHelp: Only 1 in 12 records reports a status. Filtering by status leaves the rest out.
filters.methodHelp: The source reports no procedure on 7 of every 10 records.
filters.categoryHelp: Only purchases of this article. This is the population the price comparisons use.
filters.categoryCodeHelp: Only purchases with this catalogue code — exactly the reference the price was compared against.
filters.productHelp: Filter contracts by one or more catalogue articles. Applied by exact code.
filters.hasAmountHelp: Some official records publish no amount.
filters.tagHelp: Each stage of the process is its own record. Only the award reports a supplier and an amount.

notFound.title: No contract with that identifier
notFound.body: The identifier may have changed at the source. Try searching for it in the explorer.

contract.officialSourceHelp: Opens the official record for this contract on comprasestatales.gub.uy.
contract.verifiedTotalHelp: The raw feed multiplied the quantity by a value that was actually the contract's total amount. We corrected the total against the official record; the lines below show the raw feed data.

ai.title: AI review of the price flag

contract.itemsHelp: Awarded items with quantity and unit price.
contract.noItems: The official record lists no items for this contract.
contract.noDocuments: No documents are published for this contract.
contract.rawHelp: The OCDS document as the state publishes it.

stageHelp.award: The state awarded the purchase to a supplier. This is the stage that reports amounts.
stageHelp.awardUpdate: A correction to an already published award.
stageHelp.tender: Published call. No supplier or amount yet.
stageHelp.tenderUpdate: A clarification or answer to questions about a tender.
stageHelp.tenderAmendment: A change to the tender conditions.

contract.noMoneyStage: This stage reports no amounts. The figure appears once the award is published.
contract.itemsTenderHelp: Items included in the tender. Prices appear only once it is awarded.
contract.referenceHelp: Each item compared against purchases of the same item over the last 36 months — the same baseline the flags use.

tax.help: Purchase total from the official site. Open-data feed prices are tax-exclusive.

reference.presNote: The open-data unit price does not carry the article's presentation (e.g. a 250 g container vs. 1 g), which can change how this comparison reads. When we can recover it, it appears next to the article; you can always check it on

products.title: What the state buys
products.lead: Every product and service in the state catalogue: who buys it, from whom, and in what quantity.
products.help: Grouped by catalogue code (not free-text, which fragments one category across spelling variants). Counts reflect every purchase; peso spend appears where the source reported an amount.

empty.title: No product matches that search
empty.body: Try part of the article name or its catalogue code.

detail.topBuyersHelp: Agencies that bought this product most often.
detail.topSuppliersHelp: Suppliers that sold this product to the state most often.
detail.priceHelp: Price distribution per unit and currency over the last 36 months — the same baseline the price flags use.
detail.concentrationHelp: How few suppliers account for this product's purchases: few suppliers signals a captive market.
detail.dispersionHelp: Unit-price range per currency and unit (p25 – p50 – p95). The wider the bar, the more what's paid varies.
detail.variesTitle: Is it always the same product?
detail.variesYes: The same catalogue code appears with different details across contracts (brand, presentation, or commercial name): it is not always the same unit product.
detail.variesNo: Across the contracts with characteristics available, the product is presented the same way.
detail.viewAllContracts: View all contracts for this product

notFound.title: We couldn't find that product
notFound.body: The catalogue code may not exist or may have no recorded purchases.

error.title: We couldn't load this product
error.body: There was a temporary problem reading the data. Please try again in a few seconds.

suppliers.title: State suppliers
suppliers.lead: {total} companies and individuals that sold to the Uruguayan state.

empty.title: No supplier matches that search
empty.body: Try part of the name, without the legal form: “saceem” rather than “SACEEM S.A.”.

detail.byBuyerHelp: Agencies ranked by amount awarded to this supplier.
detail.concentrationHelp: How much of their revenue comes from their largest buyer.
detail.dataNote: The source publishes no breakdown by year, agency or category for this supplier — only the total and its contracts.

notFound.title: No supplier with that identifier
notFound.body: Try searching for it in the directory.

contacts.title: Provider contact directory
contacts.lead: Contact data for {total} state providers, for research and business outreach. Download the filtered list as CSV, Excel, JSON or vCard.

download.label: Download {count} contacts
download.capWarning: The download includes the first {cap} contacts. Narrow the filters to export a smaller group.

empty.title: No contact matches those filters
empty.body: Try widening the filters or turning off “verified emails only”. The department and size filters use the DEI registry, which does not cover every provider.

notice.source: Data gathered from public sources: the MIEM DEI registry, providers' own websites, and open data from Compras Estatales. Third-party data restricted by its terms of use is excluded.
notice.optout: Are you a provider and want your data removed? Email us at

buyers.title: Buying agencies
buyers.lead: {total} Uruguayan state agencies that run procurement.

empty.title: No agency matches that search
empty.body: Try part of the name: "salud" instead of "Dirección General de la Salud".

detail.yearsHelp: Every year this agency recorded purchases. Tap a year to see those contracts.
detail.recordHelp: Amounts by year, supplier and category come from the public contract record, which only reports figures from {year} onwards. The total above comes from the agency summary and covers every year.
detail.bySupplierHelp: Suppliers ranked by amount received from this agency.
detail.bySupplierNote: Each contract’s amount is credited in full to every awarded supplier: the source does not split it between them, so on multi-supplier contracts these figures are an upper bound.
detail.categoriesHelp: Categories with the most recorded spending, by the classification on each awarded item.
detail.concentrationHelp: How much of their spending goes to their largest supplier.
detail.contractsHelp: This agency’s largest contracts in the public record.
detail.noAmounts: The public record reports no amounts for this agency.

notFound.title: No agency with that identifier
notFound.body: Try searching for it in the list.

anomalies.title: Price flags
anomalies.lead: Contracts where the unit price departs sharply from what the state usually pays for the same item. A flag is not an accusation: it marks something worth a look.
anomalies.disclaimer: There are legitimate explanations: urgent purchases, different specifications under the same catalogue code, or items misclassified at the source.

method.title: How it is calculated
method.body: For each combination of item, currency and unit of measure, a price baseline is built from the last 36 months. Each award's unit price is then scored against that baseline using a robust measure in log space: it uses the median rather than the mean, so a huge contract cannot mask its own anomaly.
method.scope: At least 10 comparable items are needed to score a price, and 30 to assign the highest severities.
method.rangeNote: The "usual range" runs from the 25th to the 95th percentile of that baseline, in the contract's original currency.

severityHelp.critical: More than 10 robust deviations above the baseline.

typeHelp.price_spike: The unit price departs from what is usually paid for that item.

anomalies.zHelp: Robust deviation of the unit price from what the same catalogue code usually costs (measured against its own recent baseline, so it is not distorted by inflation between years).

empty.title: No flags for these filters
empty.body: Try another severity or clear the year filter.

ai.label: AI review
ai.note: AI triage (Gemini) over the statistical flag: advisory only, it does not replace human judgement. Always verify against the official source.
ai.usedFeatures: {n} characteristics read from the official page

feedback.reasonPlaceholder: Why? Tell us what does (or doesn't) explain this flag.
feedback.error: Couldn't save your vote. Try again.
feedback.loginBody: Your vote helps the community separate real anomalies from false positives. You need an account for it to count.
feedback.loginError: Couldn't sign you in. Try again.

generic.title: This data didn't load
generic.body: The query failed. Retry; if it keeps failing, it's on us.

timeout.title: The query took too long
timeout.body: Add a year or agency filter to narrow the search.

notFound.title: This page doesn't exist
notFound.body: The link may be broken, or the content moved.

rateLimit.title: Too many queries
rateLimit.body: Wait a minute and try again.

unexplained.title: purchases the AI could not explain
unexplained.lead: Prices far above the norm for the same article that, even after AI review, have no visible legitimate explanation. These are the strongest lead to investigate. Ordered by worst price divergence.
unexplained.method: Each alert comes from a statistical detection (a unit price that departs sharply from its catalogue code's pattern) and is then reviewed by an AI against the contract's object, the basket and the official item characteristics. These are the ones left unexplained.
unexplained.pending: {n} alerts still in AI review — the number is still updating.
unexplained.disclaimer: The AI triage is advisory and does not replace human judgement. An unexplained price is not proof of wrongdoing: there may be context the open data does not publish. Always verify against the official source.

empty.title: Nothing unexplained right now
empty.body: No alert is currently left without a plausible explanation after review.

erroresCarga.title: data-load errors in the official records
erroresCarga.lead: Prices the AI identified as data-entry mistakes — not real overpricing: a quantity of 10,000 where it should be 1, a line total loaded as the unit price, or a figure in the wrong currency. Listed here so they can be reported and fixed at the source.
erroresCarga.banner: These are data errors, not necessarily corruption. Fixing them improves the figures across the whole site.
erroresCarga.reason: Why it looks like a load error

concentration.title: Where they concentrate
concentration.note: The agencies that entered the most errors — where to start reporting.

empty.title: No data-load errors right now
empty.body: No flag is currently classified as a data-load error.

erroresCarga.disclaimer: The AI classification is indicative. A possible load error proves nothing on its own: verify against the official contract before reporting.

comoReportar.title: How to report a data error
comoReportar.dek: Public-procurement data is entered by each buying agency and published by ACCE on Compras Estatales. When you spot a load error — a quantity or price that is clearly wrong — the fix is made at the source. This guide explains who to notify and how.
comoReportar.whoTitle: Who enters and corrects the data
comoReportar.whoBody: Each state agency enters its purchases into the SICE system. The Agency for Public Procurement (ACCE) runs the comprasestatales.gub.uy platform and publishes the open data this site uses. An error in a contract is corrected by the agency that entered it; ACCE is the platform's channel.
comoReportar.step1: Open the contract on Compras Estatales from the flag's «Report» button and confirm the value (quantity, unit price, currency).
comoReportar.step2: Identify the buying agency: it entered the data and is the one that can correct it.
comoReportar.step3: Write to the agency (its front desk or procurement area) describing the error, with the contract number and the official link.
comoReportar.step4: If there is no reply or you can't find the contact, write to ACCE's help desk with the same detail.
comoReportar.step5: Use the pre-filled text from the «Report» button: it already includes the contract, the agency and a description of the error, ready to paste.
comoReportar.attachBody: Contract number or link, the affected line, the value entered and the value that should apply, and a screenshot if you can. The more concrete, the faster it gets fixed.
comoReportar.contactsBody: Buying agency: its front desk or procurement area (shown on the agency's page). ACCE / Compras Estatales: contact channel and help desk at comprasestatales.gub.uy.
comoReportar.disclaimer: This site does not correct the official data or forward reports: it only detects possible errors and shows you where to take them. The correction is made at the source.

analyticsHub.title: The state of spending, up close
analyticsHub.dek: Every detection and analysis tool in one place: price flags, what has no explanation, load errors to report, flagged providers and spending by agency.

alertas.title: Price flags
alertas.dek: Contracts with a unit price far above the usual for the same item.

unexplained.title: Unexplained
unexplained.dek: The flags not even the AI review could justify. The strongest signal.

erroresCarga.title: Data-load errors
erroresCarga.dek: Data mistakes (quantities, currency, price) to report and fix at the source.

providerAnomalies.title: Flagged providers
providerAnomalies.dek: Which providers and buyers concentrate the unexplained flags.

providerLoadErrors.title: Recurring errors
providerLoadErrors.dek: Which providers and agencies repeat the same data-load errors.

intendencias.title: Departmental governments
intendencias.dek: Spending by the departmental governments, compared per capita.

organismos.title: Spending by agency
organismos.dek: How much each type of state agency spends.

mapa.title: Spending map
mapa.dek: A treemap of all spending, each agency sized by what it spends.

estadisticas.title: Statistics
estadisticas.dek: The spending figures: how much, on what, to whom.

comoReportar.title: How to report
comoReportar.dek: The guide to take a load error to whoever can correct it.

reportDialog.title: Report this data-load error
reportDialog.intro: This value looks like a data-entry mistake, not real overpricing. Report it to the agency that entered it so it gets corrected at the source.
reportDialog.openContract: View the contract on the site

provAnom.title: Providers behind the unexplained flags
provAnom.lead: We cross-reference the overprice flags the AI could not justify against the providers that receive them, to see whether one provider or one buyer concentrates many anomalies. Not an accusation — a place to start looking.

method.title: How it's built
method.body: A flag is 'unexplained' when the unit price paid extremely exceeds the usual range for the same item and a second AI review (Gemini) found no legitimate reason. Here we group them by provider and by buyer. Recomputed every 24 hours.
method.overprice: Overprice is an estimate: (price paid − top of the usual range) × quantity, brought to today's pesos — foreign currency is converted at the contract month's rate, then inflation-adjusted via the Unidad Indexada, so years and currencies compare on one figure.
method.clamped: {n} flag(s) with an implausible quantity were capped so a bad record can't dominate the totals.

provAnom.captiveHelp: Captive: all of the provider's flags come from a single buyer.

patterns.title: Patterns
patterns.note: What repeats behind the flags. Click any bar or pair to see those flags.

panel.concentrationSub: The providers with the most unexplained flags.
panel.buyersSub: Buyers with the most flags, and across how many providers they spread.
panel.pairsSub: The same provider and the same buyer, repeated.
panel.rubroSub: Almost all of it is medical and office supplies.

notReady.title: No cross-reference data yet
notReady.body: The per-provider computation hasn't run yet. Try again later.

empty.title: No providers for these filters
empty.body: Try lowering the minimum flags or clearing the category filter.

provLoadErr.title: Recurring data-load errors by provider and agency
provLoadErr.lead: We cross-reference the data-load errors — prices or quantities the AI flagged as a data-entry mistake, not corruption — against the providers and agencies behind them, to see whether the same provider or the same agency repeats the same error over and over. It's to report and fix at the source.

method.title: How it's built
method.body: A flag is a 'data-load error' when the extreme unit price is not a real overprice but a mistake when loading the data: a quantity of 10,000 where it should be 1, the line total loaded as the unit price, or a figure in the wrong currency. A second AI review (Gemini) classifies them. Here we group them by provider and by agency to see which ones repeat. Recomputed every 24 hours.
method.distortion: The 'mis-loaded amount' is an estimate of how much the bad record inflated the reported figure: (loaded price − top of the usual range) × quantity, brought to today's pesos. It is not money the provider billed — it is a distortion in the data — which is why it shows in celeste, not gold.
method.clamped: {n} flag(s) with an implausible quantity were capped so a bad record can't dominate the totals.

provLoadErr.captiveHelp: Concentrated: all of the provider's errors come from a single agency.

patterns.title: Patterns
patterns.note: What repeats behind the data-load errors. Click any bar or pair to see those errors.

panel.concentrationSub: The providers with the most data-load errors.
panel.buyersSub: Agencies with the most errors, and across how many providers they spread.
panel.pairsSub: The same provider and the same agency, repeated.
panel.rubroSub: Which categories carry the most load errors.

provLoadErr.disclaimer: A data-load error is not an accusation: it is bad data to fix at the source. The amounts are estimates over official open data.

notReady.title: No cross-reference data yet
notReady.body: The per-provider computation hasn't run yet. Try again later.

empty.title: No providers for these filters
empty.body: Try lowering the minimum errors or clearing the category filter.

partidos.title: Public procurement by party
partidos.lead: How each department's procurement looks, grouped by the party in office. A descriptive comparison over open data — not a management score: the data cannot attribute causes to a party.

method.title: How to read this
method.body: Each indicator is shown two ways: the median of departments (every department counts once, size-neutral) and the size-weighted aggregate. Party is public electoral record — who governed that year — never an attribution of the spending.

partidos.notReady: Per-department indicators haven't been computed yet. Run the refresh-dept-indicators job.

metricHelp.perCapita: Procurement spend divided by the department's population.
metricHelp.directShare: Direct and exception purchases, over records with a declared method. Note: a low value usually means direct purchases went unlabelled (not that fewer happened) — check the coverage.
metricHelp.priceCoverage: Records that publish an amount, over the total. Low = under-reporting, not thrift.
metricHelp.top5: Share of spend taken by the 5 largest suppliers (upper bound).
metricHelp.anomalyDensity: High-severity statistical alerts per 1,000 priced contracts.

partidos.hoverHint: Hover (or tap) a department to see its indicators.
partidos.anomalyCaveat: Alerts are statistical signals, not proven corruption. Descriptive.

cmp.title: By party: {metric}
cmp.note: Median (size-neutral) vs. aggregate (weighted). The gap between them is informative.

conf.title: How NOT to read this (confounders)
conf.size: Montevideo is 37% of the country and always FA-governed: it inflates any aggregate.
conf.geography: FA governs the urban areas, PN the rural interior: different baselines.
conf.terms: Departmental terms don't line up with presidential ones.
conf.reporting: Reporting completeness varies widely across departments.
conf.causation: Correlation isn't cause: the party in office doesn't by itself explain the spending.

anticipacion.title: Tender anticipation
anticipacion.subtitle: When a public agency is likely to publish its next tender in a given category, estimated from its own purchasing history.

disclaimer.title: This is an estimate, not an announcement
disclaimer.body: This calculation is built from each agency's historical purchasing pattern. It is not an official government announcement and not a guarantee that the tender will be published: Uruguay's procurement system does not announce tenders in advance. Read it as a probability, not a fact.

card.title: Likely upcoming tender in this category
card.disclaimer: Estimate from historical purchasing pattern, not an official announcement.

anticipacion.overdueHelp: This tender should already have been published based on the agency's historical cadence: it could appear any time now, not on the window's exact date.
anticipacion.confidenceHelp: How repeated and regular the historical buying pattern is: High = stable, recent cadence; Medium = a visible cadence with more variation; Low = a weak pattern, close to the display minimum.
anticipacion.coverage: Coverage: only agency × category pairs with at least 3 comparable historical tenders. Excludes centralized purchases, framework agreements and contract extensions.

notReady.title: Anticipation isn't available yet
notReady.body: This is built by a monthly job; if it hasn't run yet, there's no data to show.

noMatches.title: We couldn't find any upcoming tenders for that selection
noMatches.body: We searched the whole set of expected tenders and none match the chosen agencies or categories. Try a different selection or clear the filters.

mandate.transition: Transition year: the government changed mid-year; the administration that governed most of the year is shown.
mandate.boardSplit: Board seats are shared (coparticipación): the opposition typically holds 1–2 seats. This shows the national government of the day, not who runs the body.
mandate.singleSource: The 2005–2010 term is single-source; subject to verification.
mandate.disclaimer: Historical context (public electoral record); it does not imply responsibility for the spending.

timeline.title: Administrations by term
timeline.note: Who led the body in each term. Public electoral record, shown as context.

intend.title: The Intendencias, per head
intend.lead: How much each departmental government spends on public procurement, and how much that is per resident. The per-person figure evens out Montevideo's size and shows who spends well above or below the national line.
intend.seeInvestigation: Read the investigation: Intendencia de Montevideo

method.title: How it's calculated
method.body: We sum the purchases each Intendencia publishes on Compras Estatales (amount in pesos, normalised). Per-capita spend divides that total by the department's population.
method.interannual: The view opens on the latest full year and compares each department against the year before. The running year is still partial, so it's never the default; pick "All-time" for the cumulative total.
method.cap: {n} records with impossible amounts (source quantity errors) are excluded so they don't distort the sums; the rest is exactly as the State publishes it.
method.coverage: Mind the coverage: some Intendencias publish little or nothing on Compras Estatales, so a low per-capita figure can mean under-reporting, not thrift. Lavalleja and Soriano have no priced purchases and don't appear.

year.label: Year
year.partialNote: {year} is a running year: partial data.

empty.title: No spending data
empty.body: There are no priced purchases for the Intendencias right now.

panels.title: How the spending is distributed
panels.note: Everything below is computed over published purchases; the explorer has the detail for each agency.

panel.perCapitaSub: Pesos spent per person in each department. Montevideo in blue.
panel.totalSub: The absolute amount, where Montevideo dominates by size.
panel.trendSub: Purchases of all Intendencias, by year.
panel.trendHint: Tap a bar to filter the page to that year.
panel.distributionSub: Departments that deviate most from the national average of {line} per resident.
panel.interannualSub: {year} vs {prev}: which Intendencias raised and cut spending the most.

organismos.title: State spending, by public body
organismos.lead: Public procurement grouped by type of body: departmental governments, ministries, health, state enterprises and education. Who spends most and how spending evolves.
organismos.groupAria: Pick a group of public bodies
organismos.toIntendencias: See the per-resident breakdown of the Intendencias

method.title: How it's calculated
method.body: We sum the purchases published on Compras Estatales (amount in pesos, normalised), grouped by body. Ministries aggregate all their executing units; enterprises and hospitals are shown individually.
method.cap: {n} records with impossible amounts (source quantity errors) are excluded; the rest is exactly as the State publishes it.
method.refresh: The data is recalculated automatically once a month.
method.percapita: Per-resident spend only applies to the Intendencias, which have a clear population behind them. For the rest, absolute spend is compared.

year.label: Year
year.partialNote: {year} is a running year: partial data.

panel.rankingSub: Procurement spend per body within the group.

empty.title: No data yet
empty.body: The monthly per-body computation hasn't run yet.

erroresCarga.title: Data-load errors in Uruguay's public procurement
erroresCarga.description: Prices and quantities that look like mistakes when loading the official Compras Estatales data, detected with AI and listed to report and fix at the source.

comoReportar.title: How to report an error in public-procurement data
comoReportar.description: A guide to reporting a data-load error on Compras Estatales: who corrects the data, who to notify and what to include.

analyticsHub.title: Analysis & flags of Uruguay's public spending
analyticsHub.description: Price flags, unexplained purchases, data-load errors, flagged providers and spending by agency. Official open data from Compras Estatales.

gastos.title: Where your money goes — Uruguay's state spending
gastos.description: Uruguay's public spending grouped as critique: who gets paid, who spends, what the state buys and what doesn't add up. With official open data from Compras Estatales.

recop.title: Compilations — Uruguay's state spending by event
recop.description: Uruguay's public spending grouped by event: festivals, public works and stories, with what each one cost, from official Compras Estatales data.

pauta.title: State advertising — Uruguay's public advertising and who collects it
pauta.description: How much the Uruguayan state spends on advertising and propaganda, which bodies pay for it, and which media and agencies receive it. Official open data from Compras Estatales.

mapa.title: Uruguay's public spending map
mapa.description: A treemap of Uruguayan state spending: each public body dimensioned by what it spends, grouped by type. Official data from Compras Estatales.

estad.title: Uruguay's public spending statistics
estad.description: Uruguay's state spending in figures: how much, on what, to whom and by supplier type. Official open data from Compras Estatales.

organismos.title: Uruguay's state spending by public body
organismos.description: Public procurement grouped by type of body: departmental governments, ministries, health, state enterprises and education. Who spends most, with official Compras Estatales data.

intendencias.title: Uruguay's Intendencias spending, per resident
intendencias.description: How much each departmental government spends on public procurement, and how much per resident. All 19 departments compared with official Compras Estatales data.

partidos.title: Public procurement by party — departmental comparison
partidos.description: A descriptive comparison of the 19 Uruguayan departments' public procurement, grouped by the governing party. Map and open data, with no management judgement.

anticipacion.title: Tender anticipation — when each agency might publish next
anticipacion.description: An estimate of when each public agency might publish its next tender in each category, calculated from its purchasing history. Not an official announcement.

home.title: Con la tuya, contribuyente — Uruguay's public procurement
home.description: Explore {count} Uruguayan state contracts: who buys, who sells, and how much is paid. Official open data, searchable and comparable.

contracts.title: Uruguayan state contract explorer
contracts.description: Search and filter Uruguay's public contracts by agency, supplier, amount, year, and procedure type.

contractDetail.description: {buyer} awarded {amount} to {supplier} on {date}. Full contract detail, items, and official documents.

products.title: What the Uruguayan state buys — products and services
products.description: Explore every product in the state catalogue: who buys it, from which suppliers, and at what price. Official open data.

productDetail.title: {name} — state purchases

suppliers.title: Suppliers to the Uruguayan state
suppliers.description: Directory of {total} suppliers to the Uruguayan state, with amounts received, buying agencies, and contracts.

contacts.title: Contacts of suppliers to the Uruguayan state
contacts.description: Downloadable directory of contact data for {total} suppliers to the Uruguayan state: email, website, phone and category. CSV, Excel, JSON and vCard.

contactos.description: Directory of official purchasing contacts by agency of the Uruguayan state: who to write to at each contracting unit.

buyers.title: Buying agencies of the Uruguayan state
buyers.description: Directory of Uruguay's public agencies, with total spending, suppliers, and contracts.

anomalies.title: Flags in Uruguay's public procurement
anomalies.description: Uruguayan state contracts that depart from the usual pattern of price, supplier, or frequency.

unexplained.title: Unexplained overprices — Uruguay's public procurement
unexplained.description: Uruguayan state purchases paid far above the usual price that, after an AI review, have no visible legitimate explanation.

providerAnomalies.title: Providers with unexplained overprices — Uruguay
providerAnomalies.description: Which providers and buyers concentrate the overprices the AI could not explain in Uruguay's public spending. Cross-reference updated every 24 hours.

providerLoadErrors.title: Recurring data-load errors by provider and agency — Uruguay
providerLoadErrors.description: Which providers and agencies repeat the same data-load errors (quantities, currency, price) in Uruguay's public spending. Cross-reference updated every 24 hours.

curros.title: Cases on record — corruption in Uruguayan public procurement, cross-referenced
curros.description: Documented corruption cases in Uruguay’s public procurement — surfaced by the press, audits and the courts — each cross-referenced with official procurement data and its sources.

currosDetail.title: {title} — the case, cross-referenced with the data

about.title: How it works
about.lead: Where this data comes from and what you can do with it.
about.sourceBody: Everything you see here comes from the open data the Uruguayan state publishes in OCDS format (Open Contracting Data Standard), through catalogodatos.gub.uy and the Compras Estatales site. We don't add or edit records: we import them, normalize them, and make them searchable.
about.scaleBody: Every amount carries a logarithmic bar beneath it. It uses the same scale across the site, so you can compare one contract to another without reading the digits. Gold is reserved for money: if it isn't public money, it isn't gold.
about.totalTitle: Why we don't show a total
about.totalBody: A handful of official records carry corrupt quantities, and that breaks any sum. The clearest case: a UTE award for generator equipment rental declares a quantity of 1,200,007 units at USD 519,788.85 each. That comes to USD 623 billion — about eight times the country's GDP — for a single contract.
about.totalBody2: Three records like that hold 86% of all reported spending; thirteen hold 93%. A "total awarded" headline would, in practice, be the sum of those errors. So we show the median instead: half of the state's purchases are smaller than that figure. It doesn't break when one record does.
about.totalBody3: We don't delete them. Filter to that agency and you'll see the record exactly as the state publishes it, with a link to the official file. The strange number is part of the story.
about.limitsBody: Some official records report no amount, and amounts in foreign currency are shown with their original currency. A contract carrying a flag isn't necessarily irregular: it's a contract that departs from the pattern and is worth a look.
about.linkBody: Every contract links to its official record on comprasestatales.gub.uy. If something here doesn't add up, the source wins.
about.authorBody: This is a personal, independent project maintained by Eduardo Airaudo. Non-commercial and open source. It is not an official site of the Uruguayan state. If you'd like to get in touch — a question, a bug, or about your personal data — the address is below.

footer.disclaimer: Independent site. Not an official site of the Uruguayan state.

consent.text: We use measurement cookies (Google Analytics) to learn which pages are useful. The site works the same without them.

legalPage.privacyDesc: What data we process, with whom, and what rights you have. An independent project on Uruguay's public procurement.
legalPage.termsDesc: Terms of use for the site: what it is, what it is not, no warranty, and how to cite the data.

cookiesPage.title: Cookie policy
cookiesPage.desc: What the site stores in your browser, why, and how to change your decision about measurement.
cookiesPage.intro: We store a few things in your browser. Most are necessary for the site to work (your theme, your session, your progress). Only the measurement ones depend on your consent.
cookiesPage.howUnset: Undecided: Google Analytics writes no cookie and sends only identifier-less measurement. We show the notice until you choose.
cookiesPage.howGranted: If you accept: full measurement is enabled and Google Analytics' _ga cookies are written.
cookiesPage.howDenied: If you reject: the Google Analytics script is not loaded at all and no request is made to Google.
cookiesPage.moreBody: The detail of what data we process and your rights is in the

errorPage.lead404: The address doesn't exist or has moved. Try searching for a contract or going back home.
errorPage.leadGeneric: We had a problem loading this page. You can retry from the home page.
errorPage.searchPlaceholder: Search a contract, agency or supplier…

donation.help: Give it a star on GitHub or donate — it keeps the site free and up to date.

auth.subtitle: State tender alerts for your line of business.
auth.magicSent: We sent a link to {email}. Open it on this device to sign in.
auth.resetSent: If the email exists, we sent password reset instructions.
auth.callbackError: We couldn't confirm the link. Try signing in again.
auth.genericError: We couldn't complete that. Check your details and try again.
auth.verifyNotice: We sent a verification email. Verify your account to activate alerts.

contactPanel.title: Contracting unit contact
contactPanel.note: Official contact published by the agency on comprasestatales.gub.uy.

contactos.title: State purchasing contacts
contactos.lead: Who to write to at each agency: the official purchasing contact published in the open calls.

empty.title: No contact matches that search
empty.body: Try part of the agency name, the person, or the email.

contactos.note: Official contact data published by the agencies on comprasestatales.gub.uy.

llamados.title: Open calls
llamados.lead: State tenders and purchases whose submission deadline is still open.
llamados.createAlertCta: Create an alert for this search
llamados.loginToSave: Sign in to save the call and get alerts.
llamados.summaryUnavailable: The document summary isn't available yet.
llamados.summaryNoPliego: This call has no attached document to summarize.
llamados.benchmarksLead: What the State awarded these categories for, which firms won before, and where to see the detail — to prepare your bid.
llamados.benchmarksEmpty: No price history for these categories.
llamados.benchDisclaimer: Reference prices computed from the category's historical awards. Each supplier's figure is the total the State awarded them in this category. Always verify the official bidding document.
llamados.estimateTitle: How much to bid to win?
llamados.estimateLead: An estimate of the offer that historically won these items: the call's quantity times the unit price the State awarded in each category. A starting point for building your proposal.
llamados.estimateCoverage: Estimated over {estimated} of {total} items with comparable history.
llamados.estimateDisclaimer: Indicative estimate from historical awards of the same category and unit. It does not account for specifications, deadlines or terms in the bidding document, which can change the price. Always verify the official document before bidding.

hook.slotLead: In the meantime, the site already helps you:
hook.empresaV1: Get notified the moment a call in your line of business opens.
hook.empresaV2: See what the State has paid before you set your price.
hook.ciudadanoV1: Follow the money: who buys, from whom, for how much.

alerts.title: My alerts
alerts.lead: Define your line of business and we'll email you when a matching call appears.
alerts.name: Alert name
alerts.categoriesHint: Search and pick the categories you care about.
alerts.productsHint: Search or browse the official procurement catalogue and add whole categories (they cover all their articles) or individual articles.
alerts.valueHint: The range only excludes calls whose published amount falls outside it; calls with no published amount still show.
alerts.keywordsHint: Press Enter to add. Matches ignore case and accents.
alerts.buyersHint: Restrict to one or more agencies.
alerts.empty: You have no alerts yet. Create your first one.
alerts.capReached: You've reached the alert limit. Delete one to create another.
alerts.previewNone: Doesn't match any open call right now
alerts.needTrigger: Define at least one category, keyword or agency.

calendar.title: Calendar
calendar.lead: Upcoming deadlines for calls you saved and calls matching your alerts.

accountPage.title: My account
accountPage.channelsTitle: How you want to get alerts
accountPage.pushUnsupported: Your browser does not support push notifications.
accountPage.pushBlocked: You blocked notifications. Enable them in your browser settings.
accountPage.telegramNotConfigured: Telegram is not available right now.
accountPage.telegramOpenHint: Telegram will open to confirm the link.
accountPage.installHint: Add the app to your home screen.

apiKeys.title: API keys
apiKeys.lead: Keys to use the API from Zapier, MCP or other integrations.
apiKeys.intro: Create a key, store it somewhere safe, and send it in the x-api-key header or as Authorization: Bearer. Read keys query data; write keys can also manage your alerts and saved calls.
apiKeys.labelHint: A name to recognise it (e.g. "Zapier production").
apiKeys.scopeWrite: Write — manage alerts and saved calls
apiKeys.createdWarning: Copy it now: for security it won't be shown again.
apiKeys.revokeConfirm: Revoke this API key? Integrations using it will stop working.
apiKeys.empty: You don't have any API keys yet.
apiKeys.createError: We couldn't create the key. Please try again.

developers.title: Public API for state procurement
developers.lead: Integrate tenders, contracts, analytics and alerts into your own tools, into Zapier, or into an MCP client.
developers.step1Body: Create a key in your account. Read keys are optional and raise your rate limit; write keys manage alerts.
developers.step2Body: Send the key in the x-api-key header or as Authorization: Bearer.
developers.step3Body: Wire up polling triggers, webhooks, or the official MCP server.
developers.zapierBody: Point any OpenAPI-aware tool at /openapi.json. For "something new" triggers use the /api/v1/*/changes endpoints.
developers.webhooksBody: Subscribe an HTTPS URL to receive push events: a new matching tender, a new anomaly, a new award. HMAC-signed.
developers.mcpBody: Connect the API to Claude or other MCP clients with the official package, authenticated with your API key.

webhooks.title: Webhooks
webhooks.lead: Receive push events (a matching tender, an anomaly, an award) at your URL.
webhooks.intro: Subscribe an HTTPS URL. Every delivery is signed with HMAC-SHA256 in the X-GastosGub-Signature header; verify it with your secret.
webhooks.createdWarning: Copy it now: it won't be shown again. Use it to verify the HMAC signature of each delivery.
webhooks.removeConfirm: Delete this webhook? It will stop receiving events.
webhooks.empty: You don't have any webhooks yet.
webhooks.createError: We couldn't create the webhook. Check that the URL is public and https.

dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.noAlerts: Create your first alert to start receiving notices.

unsub.title: Unsubscribe
unsub.done: Done. You won't receive alert emails anymore.
unsub.error: We couldn't process the unsubscribe. The link may have expired.

picker.welcome: First time here? Let us show you around.
picker.title: What would you like to do?
picker.subtitle: Pick a guided tour. They're short and you can leave anytime.
picker.exploreDesc: Learn to explore government contracts: search, filter and read the amounts.
picker.alertsDesc: Set up an alert and get an email when a tender you care about opens.

explore.heroBody: Type a product, a company or a government body. This is where exploring public spending begins.
explore.bridgeBody: We'll take you to the explorer, where you can see every contract and filter it however you like.
explore.filtersBody: Narrow by product, buyer, supplier, amount, year or procurement method. Every filter updates the results instantly.
explore.kpisBody: For the chosen filters this summary shows how many contracts there are, what they add up to and the typical amount. The gold bar measures magnitude in pesos.
explore.tableBody: Each row is a contract. Tap one to see the detail: what was bought, from whom and for how much.
explore.doneBody: You can also check anomalies, suppliers and buyers from the menu. To replay this tour, hit the help button (?).

alerts.searchBody: These are the currently open tenders. Search by topic or keyword to find the ones you care about.
alerts.cardBody: It shows the government body, the procurement method and the closing date. Tap it for full details.
alerts.ctaBody: With an alert you don't have to check by hand: we email you when a tender matching your search opens.
alerts.loginBody: Alerts and emails are free — you just need an account to save them. Sign in and we'll pick up where we left off.
alerts.nameBody: A name helps you recognize the alert later. For example: «Computers for schools».
alerts.productsBody: Search the product or category you want to watch. Add several — the alert fires on any of them.
alerts.keywordsBody: Add words that must appear in the tender. Great for topics that don't fit a category.
alerts.refineBody: Optional: limit by body, procurement method or amount range so you only get what really matters.
alerts.previewBody: Before saving, see how many tenders match today. Tune the alert so it's neither too broad nor too narrow.
alerts.saveBody: Done. From now on we'll compare every new tender against your alert. Now let's set up email.
alerts.emailBody: Alerts arrive at this address. Important: it must be verified; if you see «unverified», check your inbox and confirm.
alerts.enableBody: Flip this switch to receive alerts by email.
alerts.freqBody: Instantly (the moment it appears) or a daily digest with everything from the day.
alerts.finishBody: Save the changes and you'll start getting notices. Create as many alerts as you like from «My alerts».

curros.indexLead: Public-procurement schemes surfaced by the press, audits or the courts — each cross-referenced with what Uruguay’s official procurement data shows.
curros.disclaimer: Each case cites public sources and its legal status. The figures are the cross-reference with procurement data: what the state spent with the people and companies named — not proof of a crime.
curros.teaserBody: Corruption cases in state procurement, each with its legal status, its sources and the contract-by-contract cross-reference with the data.
curros.suppliersHelp: Suppliers ranked by amount awarded in the cross-reference. A multi-supplier contract’s amount is counted in full for each: a ceiling, not a split.
curros.categoriesHelp: Spend by category, from each awarded item’s price times quantity, in the contract currency.
curros.ledgerHelp: Each contract in the cross-reference, largest first. Tap one for its record and the link to the official purchase.
curros.ledgerNote: Amounts in pesos, normalized. Records with impossible quantities are excluded from the total.
curros.methodBody: Each case is a saved query over the open data — a state entity, a company, a keyword — plus its legal status and its sources. The site resolves the query live, so the figures stay current. The set of contracts is an editorial cross-reference with what the sources reported, not an accusation.

notFound.title: Case not found
notFound.body: It may have been renamed or not exist yet.

inbox.title: Notifications
inbox.lead: The tenders that matched your alerts.
